# Copilot Instructions for Learning Artifacts

This repository is a growing collection of UI-based learning artifacts. Each new artifact should feel like part of a larger evolving learning gallery.

## Repository Purpose

- Keep the project focused on interactive, visual, and educational HTML-based experiments.
- Prefer simple, clean, polished single-page experiences over overly complex setups.
- Treat each artifact as a standalone learning experience that can be shared through GitHub Pages.

## Style Expectations

- Use modern, minimal, and visually appealing UI.
- Aim for a futuristic or machine-learning-inspired aesthetic when appropriate.
- Keep the experience responsive and easy to understand.
- Prefer clear typography, subtle animations, and thoughtful spacing.

## When Adding a New Artifact

1. Create a new folder for the artifact, usually named after the topic.
2. Add an HTML file that can run independently.
3. Keep the artifact self-contained when possible.
4. Make sure the artifact is easy to open and understand from a browser.
5. Update the landing page at the repository root so the new artifact appears in the gallery.

## Landing Page Rules

- The main landing page should remain the entry point for all artifacts.
- New artifacts should be added to the list with:
  - a title
  - a short description
  - a link to the artifact page
  - a matching visual style
- Preserve the overall modern and educational tone of the site.

## Content Guidelines

- Write concise, friendly descriptions.
- Use terminology that fits learning, exploration, and AI/ML concepts.
- Keep the content accessible to someone who is curious and new to the topic.

## Preferred Approach

- Favor clean HTML, CSS, and small amounts of JavaScript.
- Avoid unnecessary dependencies.
- Keep files easy to browse and maintain.

When updating this repository, make sure new changes stay consistent with this purpose and visual style.
